Section 01 — The Problem

Distal Embolization Is the Silent Complication

Every interventionalist knows the frustration: excellent angiographic results — yet suboptimal perfusion. The culprit is debris you can't see, migrating into microvasculature that standard imaging never captures.

100%
Atherectomy procedures produce downstream embolic debris — without exception.
55%
Peripheral interventions result in embolic events threatening the microvasculature.
Note: Angiography reveals only a fraction of the leg's vessels — missing the microvasculature that drives perfusion and complications.

Section 02 — The Solution

Vortex-Assisted Aspiration

Deployed after primary treatment, the RoVo™ System combines rotational agitation with active aspiration — mobilizing and evacuating embolic debris before it reaches the microvasculature.

Step 01
Rotate
Precision rotational agitation disrupts and mobilizes adherent embolic material from the vessel wall and debris matrix.
Step 02
Agitate
Controlled mechanical agitation suspends liberated debris in the blood column — conditioning it for evacuation.
Step 03
Aspirate
Active aspiration evacuates the suspended debris — preventing downstream migration into the microvasculature.

Curved Tip — Designed to Optimize Debris Engagement

Purpose-engineered tip geometry positions the aspiration port for maximal debris capture in vessel curves and bifurcations common in PAD anatomy.

📄 Now Published

Temporary Occlusion Embolectomy (TOE) for Distal Embolic Management

A peer-reviewed case series demonstrating RoVo in plaque-modifying PAD interventions. Published in Vascular Disease Management, March 2026 (E29–E39).

Davis T, et al.  |  Editorial: Dr. Craig Walker

Read the Full Publication →
